data.title: Dan Loeb's Third Point exits Bath & Body Works, enters Capital One among trades in Q4
data.description:
Third Point's Q4 2024 moves included exiting its stake in Bath & Body Works (NYSE:BBWI) after holding about 11.79M shares in the previous quarter, according to the hedge fund’s latest 13F filing.

The firm founded by Daniel Loeb also exited Apple (AAPL) by offloading 930K shares, according to its regulatory filing for the quarter ended December 31, 2024, published Friday. It left CVS (CVS) after holding ~1.57M shares in Q3, and stepped out of stakes in Amphenol (APH) and in Roper (ROP).

Third Point entered into stakes in financial companies Capital One (NYSE:COF) and Discover (DFS), purchasing 925K and 640K shares, respectively. It also picked up equity holdings in Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO), Fortive (FTV) and Workday (WDAY).

The hedge fund increased its stake in Tesla (TSLA), to 500K shares from 400K, and in Meta (META), to 665K from 545K. It also bolstered holdings in Telephone and Data Systems (TDS), LPL Financial (LPLA), and sports betting company Flutter Entertainment (FLUT).

It reduced stakes in Microsoft (MSFT), Cinemark (CNK), Vistra (VST), Danaher (DHR) and Apollo Global (APO).

data.title: Michael Burry's Scion adds Estee Lauder, exits Shift4, among Q4 trades
data.description:
Scion Asset Management, run by Michael Burry of "The Big Short" fame, took new stakes in Estee Lauder (NYSE:EL), PDD Holdings (NASDAQ:PDD), and HCA Healthcare (NYSE:HCA), and closed out positions in Shift4 Payments (NYSE:FOUR), Olaplex Holdings (OLPX), and The RealReal (REAL) during Q4 2024, it disclosed in its 13F filing on Friday.

The firm added 100K shares of Estee Lauder (NYSE:EL) for a stake valued at ~$7.50M at Dec. 31, 2024; 75K shares in PDD (NASDAQ:PDD) with a value of $7.27M; and 15K shares in HCA Healthcare (NYSE:HCA) with a value of $4.50M.

The fund increased its holdings in American Coastal (ACIC) to 146K shares (value ~$1.97M) from 100K shares.

It reduced its stakes in JD.com (JD) to 300K shares (value $10.4M) from 500K shares; Alibaba (BABA) to 150K shares (value $12.7M) from 200K; and Molina (MOH) to 25K shares (value ~$7.28M) from 30K shares, according to the filing.

data.title: Glenview Capital adds Microsoft, exits Marvell, Seagate, others
data.description:
Glenview Capital added Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Centene (CNC) with 1.29M shares, and Pinterest (PINS) in its latest quarter, according to a 13F filing.

The hedge fund exited stakes in Marvell (NASDAQ:MRVL), Seagate (STX), and Match Group in Q4, according to the filing on Friday.

Glenview boosted its stakes in ZoomInfo (ZI), Viatris (VTRS), and Alight (ALIT), adding 14M shares, and Myriad Genetics (MYGN) in Q4.

The fund decreased its stakes in Cigna (CI), Corteva (CTVA), and Clarivate (CLVT), shedding 7.7M shares, according to the 13F filing.

data.title: TechPrecision announce CEO as interim principal financial officer
data.description:
• TechPrecision (NASDAQ:TPCS) board appointed Alexander Shen, the chief executive officer, to serve as the interim principal financial officer and principal accounting officer of the company, effective February 14, 2025, after the previously disclosed resignation of Richard Roomberg, the company's former CFO.
  • The company is unable to file its quarterly report for the period ended December 31, 2024, within the prescribed time period.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Orvana Minerals announces director appointments
data.description:
• Orvana Minerals (TSX:ORV:CA) announced that Michael Davies, Alfredo Garcia Gonzalez, Robert Metcalfe and Michael Mutchler were elected as directors of the company. 
  • Robert Metcalfe was re-appointed as chairman of the board. 
  • The company will also re-appoint PricewaterhouseCoopers as the company's independent auditor for the ensuing year. 
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Corvex takes new bitcoin bet with IBIT, exits Hexcel, among Q4 top moves
data.description:
Keith Meister's Corvex Management took new stakes Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), with 201K shares, and iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT), with 1.18M shares, during Q4 2024, the hedge fund disclosed in a 13F filing Friday.

Meanwhile, it closed its position in Hexcel Corp. (HXL) during the three-month period.

The firm also boosted its holdings in CSX Corp. (CSX), to 1.75M shares from 1.04M shares, IAC Inc. (IAC), to 2.41M shares from 1.49M shares, and Algonquin Power & Utilities Corp.(AQN), to 3.35M shares from 3.04M shares.

And it lowered its stake in Illumina (ILMN), to 1.7M shares from 2.28M shares.

data.title: Eminence Capital adds GE Healthcare, exits Smartsheet, among Q4 trades
data.description:
Eminence Capital added GE Healthcare (NASDAQ:GEHC), Advance Auto (NYSE:AAP), and Freshworks (NASDAQ:FRSH) to its portfolio in Q4 2024, while exiting Smartsheet (SMAR), CBRE (NYSE:CBRE), and Monday.com (NASDAQ:MNDY), it disclosed in its 13F filing on Friday.

The firm acquired 1.31M shares of GE Healthcare (NASDAQ:GEHC) with a value of $102.6M at Dec. 31, 204; 1.84M shares of Advance Auto (NYSE:AAP) with a value of $87.2M; and ~5.32M shares of Freshworks (NASDAQ:FRSH) with a value of $86.0M.

The company increased its stakes in Elevance (ELV) to 555.5M shares (value $204.9M) from 231K; Atmus Filtration (ATMU) to 4.59M shares (value $179.9M) from 2.56M shares; and Ferguson Enterprises (FERG) to ~778K shares (value $135.0M) from ~296K.

Among stakes it pared: Charles River (CRL) to 627K shares (value $115.8M) from 1.00M; LPL Finanicial (LPLA) to ~559K shares (value $182.4M) from ~1.08M; and Corteva (CTVA) to 2.36M shares (value $134.7M) from 3.16M, according to the filing.

data.title: Druckenmiller's Duquesne adds airlines, exits Microsoft, Broadcom, others
data.description:
Stanley Druckenmiller's Duquesne Family Office closed out holdings in Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), Broadcom (AVGO), Mid-America (MAA), and Camden Property (CPT), according to its latest 13F filing.

New positions include 1.04 million shares in United Airlines (NASDAQ:UAL), 818K in Delta Air (DAL), 2.5M in SLM Corp (SLM), and new stakes in Amazon (AMZN) and Skechers (SKX).

The firm increased positions in Teva (TEVA), US Steel (X) and Insmed (INSM), according to the Q4 filing.

The billionaire's family office reduced stakes in Coupang (CPNG) by 2.8M, Flutter (FLUT), Seagate (STX), and Freeport-McMoRan (FCX) by 618K shares, according to the filing.

data.title: Algonquin Power & Utilities appoints interim CFO
data.description:
• Algonquin Power & Utilities (TSX:AQN:CA) announced that vice president of investor relations Brian Chin will assume the role of interim chief financial officer, effective March 7, 2025.
  • The announcement follows an earlier announcement of the pending departure of current CFO, Darren Myers.
  • Algonquin has engaged a search firm to commence a search to identify a permanent CFO.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Wheat prices pop as cold weather threatens winter crops
data.description:
U.S. wheat futures surged Friday on the Chicago Board of Trade, with a lack of snow cover seen leaving U.S. winter wheat vulnerable to below-freezing temperatures.

Highs in the Plains state are expected to be well below freezing over the weekend, with subzero highs expected in North Dakota, and traders are concerned about the bitter cold killing winter wheat crops because of a lack of snow cover; however, a storm system also is forecast to move across the U.S., which could provide new snow cover for affected crops.

CBOT wheat for March delivery (W_1:COM) finished +4.1% to $6.01 1/2 per bushel, while March soybeans (S_1:COM) settled +0.7% to $10.36 3/4 per bushel and March corn (C_1:COM) closed +0.6% to $4.96 1/2 per bushel.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:WEAT), (NYSEARCA:SOYB), (NYSEARCA:CORN), (DBA), (MOO)

President Trump's lack of immediate reciprocal tariffs was taken as a positive in the U.S. grain market, since the action appears to have been aimed mostly at bringing countries to the negotiating table rather than immediate pain.

Grain futures continue to benefit from less than ideal weather conditions in Argentina, where the Rosario Grain Exchange lowered its outlook for corn production by 2M metric tons to 46M and for soybean output by 2.5M tons to 47.5M tons.

Wheat futures have surged nearly $0.60 per bushel since mid-January, with cold weather expected to cause a supply squeeze for Russian crops following a series of large crops and exports in recent years; some observers believe Russia’s current crop may be the smallest in more than a decade.

"Wheat is... finding support from reductions to the Russian crop and building concerns over the Indian crop," Karl Setzer of Consus Ag Consulting said. "India is already seeing its wheat supplies drop and now another short crop will likely lead to imports."
